root_text.iti105:2.1: “Cattārome, bhikkhave, taṇhuppādā, yattha bhikkhuno taṇhā uppajjamānā uppajjati.
root_text.iti105:2.3: Cīvarahetu vā, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno taṇhā uppajjamānā uppajjati;
root_text.iti105:2.4: piṇḍapātahetu vā, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno taṇhā uppajjamānā uppajjati;
root_text.iti105:2.5: senāsanahetu vā, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno taṇhā uppajjamānā uppajjati;
root_text.iti105:2.6: itibhavābhavahetu vā, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno taṇhā uppajjamānā uppajjati.
root_text.iti105:2.7: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro taṇhuppādā yattha bhikkhuno taṇhā uppajjamānā uppajjatī”ti.
root_text.iti105:5.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti105:0.3: The Chapter on the Brahmin’s Offering of the Teaching
translation_text.iti105:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti105:2.1: “Mendicants, there are four things that give rise to craving in a mendicant.
translation_text.iti105:2.6: or rebirth in this or that state.
translation_text.iti105:2.7: These are the four things that give rise to craving in a mendicant.”
translation_text.iti105:3.2: as they transmigrate on this long journey.
translation_text.iti105:3.3: They go from this state to another,
translation_text.iti105:4.2: that craving is the cause of suffering—
translation_text.iti105:4.3: rid of craving, free of grasping,
translation_text.iti105:5.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.
